title: Viguiera rugosa
taxonomy: Viguiera rugosa is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family includes thousands of species, many of which are familiar ornamental plants. The genus Viguiera itself is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas, and Viguiera rugosa is one of the species within this genus. parts: Viguiera rugosa, also known as the rough fleabane or Mexican gold poppy, is a plant with a history of traditional medicinal use. intro: Viguiera rugosa is a flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various health issues such as fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, with reported health benefits including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects.
